entry.title: Re: S4HANA Settlement Management : Process Overview
entry.content: Hi Nilang, I have created new pricing procedures copying the standard ones but no major changes were made to the new ones. Trust me, the standard ones would work. What was the issue that you were facing? Were you able to resolve?

entry.title: SACモデルで設定した階層をDSP側に持っていくことはできるのか？
entry.content:
SAC側でモデルを作成し、そのモデルのデータをDSP側にデータフロー経由でインポートしていたのですが、インポートされるディメンションテーブルが、SAC側のディメンションテーブルと若干ことなっているのが気になっています。

具体的には画像のように、SAC側に「店舗エリア」を階層に設定したShopのディメンションテーブルがあるのですが、それに対応していると思われるShopMasterというテーブルをDSP側でインポートすると、「店舗エリア」のカラムがなくなっているのです。

SAC側で作成した階層の設定をDSPに持っていくことはできないのでしょうか？

また、DSP側で表示されるMasterDateなどのテーブルはどのような経緯で作られているのでしょうか…？ これらのテーブルはSAC側で確認することはできるのでしょうか…？

まとまっていない質問で大変申し訳ないのですが、なにか関連している情報などをお持ちの方がいらっしゃいましたら、ぜひ共有していただきたいです。
よろしくお願いいたします。

 

【参考】
・SACに「店舗エリア」を階層に設定したShopのディメンションテーブルをもつモデルを作成する

・このモデルのディメンションテーブル（ShopMaster）をDSPにインポートする

 

・DSP側のShopのディメンションテーブルをインポートすると「店舗エリア」という列がなくなっている(「GET_POINT」というカラムがあるが、これは自動追加された座標のカラム？）

entry.title: Cause of HANA Cloud High CPU
entry.content:
Hello experts, 

Recently our HANA Cloud cpu reaches to very high percentage of usage frequently, and occring delta merge at the same time.

What might be the typical cause of such a situation?

I want to know how to research this issue.

Thanks & Regards,

Daisuke.H

entry.title: Benchmarking: Bridging the Gap Between Core Business and BPM Practice
entry.content:
In today’s rapidly evolving business landscape, organizations continuously face the challenge of enhancing business operations and customer satisfaction through innovation. Benchmarking offers a valuable strategy that addresses these challenges by comparing against industry best practices and leading peers, offering a performance baseline. This fosters a common understanding between key business stakeholders and business process management (BPM) specialists.

To streamline business transformation and improvement projects, companies need a unified language that encapsulates their focus areas and targets. Therefore, ensuring clarity in communication between business strategy and BPM is instrumental. 

Designing a Better Business Strategy with Benchmarking 

Benchmarking as a methodology has already existed for a long time. However, its corporate adoption was first pioneered by Xerox to enhance organizational performance. 

Benchmarking now pairs with process transformation to measure core business activities and compare them on a broader spectrum. This collaboration offers a basis for transformation projects, ensuring seamless interaction between business and BPM along standardized measurements. 

Benchmarking enables organizations to comprehend the concept of 'good' in their respective industries. It is this understanding and pursuit of achieving 'good' that fuels business growth and success. And when combined with a robust understanding of its elements, benchmarking provides an essential yardstick for companies to measure and improve their baseline performance. 

Deciphering the Language of Benchmarks 

Benchmarks serve as specific standards, metrics, or reference points that gauge a company's performance, efficiency, or effectiveness. They act as the mirror that reflects an organization's proficiency in handling its processes, products, or services. 

Benchmarking can be classified into internal and external types based on the comparison used. Internal benchmarking involves a comparative study within the same organization, often between different subsidiaries. In contrast, external benchmarking extends the comparison to other companies within the same industry or otherwise comparable peer groups. 

Moreover, benchmarks can be strategically divided into functional, operational, strategic, and financial categories: 

  • Functional benchmarks align with business functions. 
  • Operational benchmarks focus on internal processes and operations. 
  • Strategic benchmarks aid in measuring progress towards long-term strategic goals. 
  • Financial benchmarks help monitor a company's financial health and performance. 

Choosing the right benchmark can be challenging. It’s key that the chosen benchmark comes from a comparable peer, ensuring an authentic and valuable comparison. For this reason, measuring against standardized business processes enhances comparability across organizations, making them an ideal basis for comparison. 

Benchmarking drives businesses growth and competitiveness within the industry. By connecting core business functions and BPM, benchmarking becomes a powerful tool for driving transformation initiatives in business operations. Just as cities are built one brick at a time, businesses progress step by step with each benchmark. 

Integration with Core Business Management: 

An effective benchmarking strategy is inherently linked to the notion of business management. This strategy weighs processes at every level, thereby rendering a clear link between strategic aims and process improvements. As Peter Drucker adeptly puts it, "Management is doing things right; leadership is doing the right things". Thus, benchmarking is an insightful tool that equips businesses to identify and prioritize these "right things". 

This approach facilitates a tight-knit connection between strategic CEO-level directives and operational decision-making. A prime example of this integration lies in finance, an area ripe for benchmarking due to standardized reporting principles. These principles promote comparisons of operational effectiveness and efficiency, driving more insightful and rapid decisions. 

Linking Benchmarks with Business Process Management (BPM): 

Similar to financial departments, implementing BPM as a basis across different business functions can promote standardization, allowing for comparisons and benchmarking. BPM entails the identification, modeling, analysis, and refinement of business processes, which can then be used for benchmarking purposes. 

Leveraging standardized end-to-end processes and adding external benchmarks onto internal metrics can offer guided analysis and improvements. This integration in the SAP Signavio suite fosters the perfect synergy between BPM and business management. 

In summary, benchmarking acts as a crucial strategic compass for businesses navigating today’s highly competitive landscape. They provide invaluable insights into a company's fundamental processes, empowering organizations to enhance their operations effectively. When integrated seamlessly with business management and BPM structures, benchmarking drives performance improvement and paves the way for sustainable growth. 

For organizations aiming to make significant strides in their industries, integrating benchmarking into their core business strategies is essential. The resulting success stories could become the inspiring legends that future businesses emulate like the achievement from Xerox we just learned about!

entry.title: Re: Logic of CDC (Change Data Capture) in ABAP CDS View
entry.content: Good one Ashish. Clearly explained CDC logging concept and troubleshooting.

entry.title: Is there any possible the conversation amount go to Production overhead Gl account.
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We are working on the alternative BOM and we have created the below scenario. We are using the S4 hana public cloud and following the below process.

G-RF is the final product and G-ICR and G-HLR consumable product.

  1. We have done the Standard Cost for G-RF - $23.78 and use the G-ICR product in it’s Bill of Materials, and produced in the CHMILL resource department.
  2. We have done the Standard cost for G-HLR - 35.61 and use the G-PCR material in it’s bill of materials, and be made in the CHHEADLA resource department.
  3. G-RF, G-ICR, and G-HLR has been marked as costing relevant.  In the Material master data, under tab Costing 1, those have not the “Do Not Cost” checkbox checked. 
  4. The G-RF having the 2 production versions for making the product. 
    1. The main production version used in costing is 0001 and set to use G-ICR in the BOM, and should use the resource group CHMILL.
    2. The second production version 0002 and set to use G-HLR in the BOM, and use the resource group CHHEADLA
    3. Product Costing done by Production version 0001
  1. We have done the Plan cost and actual for G-HLR. For Actual when making the change from G-HLR to G-RF we need to use a little more G-HLR for transfer loses.  So to make 1 ton of G-RF we used 1.1 tons of G-HLR.

For example: Created order for G-RF using production Version 0002.

6)  Is there a way that we can use a production order to produce G-RF from G-HLR, without effecting the standard cost?

7)  How did we change the G-HLR material into G-RF and have the cost difference post to 530001(Production overhead account) not variance account. 

Regards,

Naveen

entry.title: Re: Search attributes cannot be determined in SAP MDG
entry.content: Hi Yurii, we are also facing the same issue in production. How did you resolve this issue ?

entry.title: Vizframe: customize tooltip for measures names
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I'm currently working with a sap.viz.ui5.controls.VizFrame control, i want to set customized tooltips for the measures here:

 

I couldn't find a property in the demo kit that sets tooltips to these fields.

 

Is there a way to set customized tooltips to these fields?

 

Best Regards,

Ala

entry.title: Re: How to upload data to date "Unassigned" 0000-00-00 in SAC

The tenant I am working on is Wrangler version 2024.10.1. Considering the Improvement Request is set to Delivered I am wondering if this is a software defect that my planning model tells me 000000 does not exist.

entry.title: Re: ABAP Native SQL not working
entry.content: Weird.

entry.title: Re: ABAP Native SQL not working
entry.content:
Except the missing colon in the "fetch" line, your program works for me.

      FETCH NEXT dbcur into :wa-matnr

entry.title: Re: SLP MDG integration setups
entry.content: Here's a link to the published configuration info.

entry.title: Implementation of Local Extensions for SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing (ICVCP3_EN_Col01)
entry.content:
We are happy to announce our next e-learning on SAP Learning Hub for customers of SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing.

This e-learning gives you an overview of our configuration and pricing services on SAP BTP with a dedicated focus on implementing extensions. This course explains the different use cases of local extensions and remote extensions and shows how to implement a local extension for the Pricing service.

Course Content

  • Giving an Overview of SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing
  • Explaining the Concept of Extension
  • Implementing Local Extensions
  • Summarizing and Breaking Down the Key Takeaways

Course Goals

  • Understand the extension concepts of SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing
  • Implement local extensions for the Pricing service

See: Implementation of Local Extensions for SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing
(you might have to click the search button again after opening the link)

Before you can access the e-learning link, a one-time registration in the SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support, is required. No additional fees are required. A detailed step-by-step guidance can be found here.

This should help new customers decide which extension approach to use and get all the information to start the implementation.

Feedback is appreciated.

entry.title: print out column names
entry.content:
for example I have a code like this : 

SELECT lokasyon ana_lokasyon FROM zmm_016_t_loksyn INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_analok).  

How do I print the column headers of the lt_analok table? 

ABAP Testing and Analysis #abap

entry.title: I am getting error in sales order while entering WBS element
entry.content:
Hi All

I am getting error in sales order posting while entering WBS element.

I am getting G/L missing error in sales order posting.

If WBS element is not given, sales order created without errors.

please provide solution for this.

entry.title: Hiding Workflow Step Names in Business Process Automation
entry.content:
Hi Expert,

Background:
 
  • We are using SAP Business Process Automation (BPA) in SAP BTP
  • In the My Inbox UI, step names associated with each task are currently displayed at the bottom of open tasks. 

  •  

Objective:

  • We want to hide the step names defined in the workflow from the UI.

Steps Taken So Far:

  1. Workflow Configuration:

    • We have explored the workflow configuration within BPA.
    • We identified the specific step/task for which we want to hide the name .

       

       

  2. UI Customization:

    • We attempted to locate the relevant UI code responsible for rendering the step names.
    • However, we couldn’t find the exact place to make the necessary adjustments.

Request:

  • Have any of you had the same problem and found a possible resolution?
  • Specifically, we want to exclude the step names from the UI while ensuring that the workflow functionality remains intact.

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Left-to-Right
entry.content:
Hello,
I have a question about Appgyver, is there a possibility of declaring "Right-to-Left writing to manipulate Arabic & Hebrew text ?
(with Text align properties « left » in the component it’s not good solution)

Thank you for your help

#appgyver #sapbuildapps

entry.title: Where can I start learning SAP Access Control?
entry.content:
We  recommend to access our Discovering the Main Functionalities of SAP Access Control learning journey.

Overview
This learning journey will introduce you to the Principles of SAP Access Control by exploring the functionalities for Access Governance.

Learning objectives
After completing this learning journey, you will acquire an understanding of SAP Access Control. You will be able to explain how SAP Access Control helps to address business challenges and identify authorization risks in typical business processes by learning how to manage access risks, access requests, roles, emergency access and by learning how to periodically review user access.

Prerequisites

  • Exploring the Principles of SAP Governance, Risk and Compliance in the S/4HANA Public Cloud

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you.

We appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

entry.title: Re: Cross Landscape Distribution - Focused Build Standalone Extensions Part 1
entry.content:
Hi @stefan_doktor,

I have created a group with 3 systems in it, 1 source and 2 target systems. When I start Wizard at the target system selection step, I can see both target systems included in the group. However, I can only select one system for distribution.

with best regards,

Alexander

entry.title: Journal Entry created with posting date outside of Normal Posting Period
entry.content:
Journal Entry created in app Post General Journal Entries  (app ID  F0718 ) with posting date outside of Normal Posting Period set in app Manage Posting Periods (App ID F2293)  Posting Date in Closed Period. 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Journal Entry created with posting date outside of No...
entry.content:
Check Adjustment Periods set in app Manage Posting Periods. Is posting date within Adjustment Periods? 

See help.sap.com documentation in relation to manage posting periods including 

Example: How to Set Up Authorizations for Posting Periods
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/41da8ed581bb481ea07a791c5ff3c350.html?locale=en-US

See also kba 2598676 - Manage Posting Periods Authorization

entry.title: Talent Intelligence Hub - Myths and Misconceptions - Part 3 (Functionality)
entry.content:
In my previous two blog posts I covered several misconceptions related to the prerequisites of and the migration to Talent Intelligence Hub (TIH), which is the latest framework for managing competencies, skills and more within SAP SuccessFactors.

The last part of this three-part series will analyze misunderstandings related to the features and functionality of Talent Intelligence Hub and provide clarity on what’s truly possible with this new framework.

All statements are true as of the latest 1H 2024 release.

 

Myth #1 – You can't attach competencies or skills to job roles anymore when you turn on TIH

While I was never able to identify the source of this myth, I can safely say it is not true. Quite the opposite applies to this scenario! Conceptually, Talent Intelligence Hub and Job Profile Builder are closely connected, as can be seen below.

 

JPB enables customers to build their entire job architecture, including job profiles, within the system. With TIH enabled, you can link competencies and skills to the job roles you created in JPB.

It is possible to manage your job architecture on the old Job Description Manager (JDM) framework and be on TIH for competencies and skills. In this case, you can’t attach skills to job roles. We highly recommend that all customers move away from JDM and enable JPB and TIH to benefit from all recent and future innovations.

 

Myth #2 - Competencies are no longer relevant

This misconception is not a technical one, but very important to address. Let’s start by saying that SAP doesn’t plan to abandon competencies or deprecate any functions related to them. Competencies have been part of our product capabilities for a very long time and most customers have embedded competencies in their talent management processes. There is no reason to move away from this as long as it adds benefit for the business and their employees.

The perception that competencies are irrelevant might stem from the strong focus on skills which was identified and discussed in our HR-meta trend analysis in 2023. Skills remain top of mind in 2024, as described in our HR meta-trend analysis in 2024. However, if your organization’s processes rely on competencies, continue to use them, and simply consider looking into how skills data, too, could help you identify, attract and retain talent.

 

Myth #3 - TIH automatically figures out your skills and competencies

While Talent Intelligence Hub is capable of a lot, it can’t determine which skills and competencies your organization needs. This will always be a process that requires a thorough analysis of what information matters the most to understand the abilities and knowledge of your organization and help you design talent processes in a way that will attract and develop top talent.

However, there is a brand-new functionality that can speed up identifying skills by analyzing existing data. With the latest release, SAP SuccessFactors introduced the AI-assisted skills architecture creation feature. This feature can create an AI-based skills library by extracting skills from job profiles in Job Profile Builder. Soon, it will also be capable of extracting skills from existing job postings managed within SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting.

 

You can easily activate this feature from within the Upgrade Center. Please carefully review the release information about the AI-Assissted Skills Architecture Creation feature before you proceed.

 

Myth #4 - TIH automatically attaches competencies and skills to job roles

The answer to this is a continuation of the response to the previous myth. The new the AI-assisted skills architecture creation feature doesn’t just extract skills, it also attaches them to the corresponding job roles. This way you can quickly build your skills library and associate your skills to your job architecture.

Please note that this only applies to skills. Competencies still need to be attached manually to job roles, which you can do one by one or as a mass change via an import.

 

Myth #5 - Career Worksheet / Career Explorer become obsolete

This misconception came up several times when presenting Growth Portfolio, the employee-facing component of Talent Intelligence Hub. I understand that Growth Portfolio might seem like the new place to perform a fit-gap analysis related to job role requirements, but it isn’t meant as a replacement.

Let’s compare the two features to see differences and commonalities:

  • Growth Portfolio is the single source of truth for everything related to competencies, skills and any other knowledge and abilities of the employee. It allows adding and updating data quickly and takes into account skills and competencies linked to the employee’s job role. The focus of this feature is to show what the employee knows right now. Most importantly. Growth Portfolio can be used fully without any job architecture in place
  • Career Worksheet / Career Explorer, on the other hand, requires a job architecture and focuses on future roles. It is specifically meant to help employees identify job roles they could develop into and show which skill or competency gaps they need to work on. While it can show gaps for the current job role, too, the emphasis lies on future roles

It’s important to understand that these features are not mutually exclusive, so you can have Growth Portfolio and Career Worksheet in place in parallel. In fact, we introduced a new experience for Career Worksheet which I like to refer to as the new blended experience for Career Worksheet, Growth Portfolio and Opportunity Marketplace. It perfectly combines these different functions on a single page to provide a one-stop shop for everything related to development and career progression.

 

Did I miss any myths or misconceptions about TIH? Please share them in the comments section and I’d be happy to comment on them.

entry.title: Re: How to upload data to date "Unassigned" 0000-00-00 in SAC
entry.content: hi @edwardwilson, seems to still work on a new/measure model here on my end with wrangler 2024.10.1. Also empty NULL values work to get it on the unassigned member. Suggest you log an incident if still facing issues.

entry.title: Re: Negative Deduction config changes not impacting on ex...
entry.content: It is resolved by maintaining the effective date as 01012024 and by maintaining the deduction rule in Over entitlement field of counting rule setup. Thank you.

entry.title: Secure Service Layer external access with signed SSL cert
entry.content:
Hi All,

the current SAPB1 server setup uses self-signed SSL cert (SLD and ServiceLayer) and the server name is just SAP

the requirement here is to make ServiceLayer available for external access to integrate with SalesForce and secure it with a signed SSL cert as SalesForce doesn't accept self-signed certs.

I understand that a valid signed SSL cert has to be bought and installed to SLD/SL, but for SAP hostname?

the remote ServiceLayer link would be https://sap.companyname.com:50000

what would be the best approach here?

Regards

GN

entry.title: PO7.5 Node functions
entry.content:
Hi ,

I am using PO7.5. I am new to node functions and need some help.

Say,I have 3 input fields(ip1, ip2, ip3) and one output field(op1).

I want to achieve below functionality with node functions or UDF. Can anyone guide me on this?

If IP1 or IP2 is initial then OP1 = IP3.

If IP1 is not initial and IP2 is not initial then OP1 = concate(IP1, IP2).

Do i need to create Custome function or with simple node functions is it possible?

Thanks,
Pavan.

So everyone who has a new job please do the same. Maybe we can create some pressure to change the process.

However, I'm not sure which (cloud) Roadmap could apply to this kind of projects? Could any of you please kindly share you suggestions? I have attached the screenshots for possible roadmaps currently available for "Cloud Specific Methodology" and "General / Cross Topics".

Thanks and kind regards,

Yi

entry.title: API doubts with SFAPI user to extract employee´s goal info
entry.content:
Hi,

We are trying to extract employee´s goal info. 


If we try to do this with a SFAPI user, we are getting an error. To get info without error, we need to put this SFAPI user as a custom manager of employee we are trying to get info.

This is a good practice? Or there is another method to get this info?

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Enabling In-Order processing with SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh Partitioned Queues
entry.content:
This blog has been co-authored by -  
 
Prashant Rakheja, SAP 
Hari Rangarajan, Solace 

This blog covers SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh’s support for Partitioned Queues (henceforth referred to as PQs) and how this feature can be used to support several critical business needs such as in-order & parallel processing of messages. It also covers a special use-case involving “exactly-once-in-order” processing of messages with SAP Cloud Integration & AEM, by leveraging PQs.

Overview of Partitioned Queues 
 
In AEM, there are two access types for Queues:

1) Exclusive 

In an exclusive Queue, only one consumer can actively connect and consume messages from the Queue, while others remain on standby.

2) Non-exclusive

In a non-exclusive Queue, multiple consumers can actively connect to the Queue and consume in a round-robin fashion.  

To learn more about configuring AEM Queues, you can refer to the AEM help doc.  

A partitioned queue is a special type of non-exclusive Queue where each partition represents a logical subdivision within the Queue.  

Key aspects of PQ:

  1. Broker distributes incoming messages to partitions in a PQ based on a ‘partition key’. All messages with the same ‘partition key’ are handled by the same partition.  

    The following table provides API information regarding how the ‘partition key’ can be set on corresponding API for each client type:  

    Client API 

     

    API Parameter 

     

    JMS 

    JMSXGroupID 

    HTTP 

    JMSXGroupID 

    MQTT5 

    JMSXGroupID 

    SMF 

    QUEUE_PARTITION_KEY 

    AMQP 

    group-id 

         For up-to-date information on details about the exact key refer to this link.

    2. If no partition key is specified, the message would be randomly distributed to any of the partitions.  

    3. Each partition connects to exactly one consumer. 

    4. Each partition in a PQ can handle more than one partition key. All messages within a partition are delivered in-order. However, there is no guarantee of message ordering across different partitions in a Queue. 

    5.One of other benefits of Partitioned Queues is that it enables parallel consumption of messages from the Queue, thereby improving egress throughput without the need for additional ‘exclusive access’ queues.


In the above diagram, there is a non-exclusive Queue that contains three partitions.  
 
G1 and G2 are messages that belong to the same group (same partition key). But H1, H2 (same group) also reside in the same partition. This point is important because while setting the partition key ensures that messages that carry the same partition key end up in the same partition, a given partition can also have messages that carry a different partition key. All the messages (in the same partition) would be delivered in-order irrespective of their group.  
 
As far as consumption is concerned, there are 2 consumers for 3 partitions (refer to the diagram above).  
 
Each partition maintains a sticky connection to the consumer, and in case the consumer goes down, connection is reconciled to another consumer. A partition can have at-most 1 active consumer, which is important from the point-of-view of maintaining in-order processing.  

Exactly-Once-In-Order (EOIO) processing 

In addition to in-order processing, if a scenario requires ‘exactly-once’ quality, it is important to understand some edge cases which cannot be handled by AEM Broker alone. 
 
AEM’s Partitioned Queues described above can be leveraged along with SAP Integration Suite’s Cloud Integration capability to build scenarios involving “exactly-once-in-order” processing. By combining the power of AEM for in-order processing and the power of Cloud Integration for ‘exactly-once’ processing, there is a possibility to handle EOIO. 
 
Why is 'exactly-once' messaging important for your business? 
 
In some cases, it is important that the messages are delivered exactly-once due to the inability of the consuming system to process duplicates, or the transaction is not differentiable as a duplicate or different transaction.  

For example, a FinTech company processing loyalty points using an event-driven-architecture -- Imagine the consequences if some of those events are delivered more than once, when they were not supposed to be.  

In any case, it's important that the middleware system can support this. 

Building EOIO scenarios in Integration Suite (Cloud Integration and AEM) 
 
With the new AEM adapter, it becomes easy to connect Cloud Integration with AEM. The AEM adapter is a key component which makes it simple to consume AEM’s native features such as Partitioned Queues etc. in Cloud Integration. It is also possible to connect to AEM with standard adapters like AMQP, but it is recommended to go with AEM adapter itself since it is tailor made for AEM.  

Publishing events via Cloud Integration   

For publishing, AEM broker provides the following APIs: JMS/AMQP/HTTP/MQTT. If there is a need to have other client types, Cloud Integration can be leveraged. The iflow below uses an HTTP endpoint for the client to publish messages. It shall be possible to use other adapters following a similar approach. 

This section is optional if AEM broker’s APIs are directly used. 

The above iFlow can be used as a reference model for publishing messages in-order to AEM. 

 

The sequence diagram above depicts how the messages flow into Cloud Integration using the HTTP adapter and get published in AEM broker using AEM adapter.  

While publishing messages to AEM broker, following steps are needed to set the ‘partition key’ in AEM adapter: 

    1. Use message property to dynamically assign the ‘partition key’ -- the actual value can be set via something like a groovy script step.


This is a small snippet where groupID is extracted from the message payload (note: the message payload needs to contain such data) and setting that as a message property to provide context to Cloud Integration.  
 
In Advanced Event Mesh adapter ‘user properties’ configuration, create a new key-value pair as shown below to set the partition key.  

Since the adapter uses SMF protocol, the key should be ‘JMSXGroupID’ -- you should use QUEUE_PARTITION_KEY (which is constant with the value ‘JMSXGroupID’) while using the programmatic client.

The most important consideration during publishing messages to cloud integration is that the client must wait for acknowledgement before publishing the next message. This is critical for ensuring that events are persisted in-order in the AEM queue.

Consuming from AEM in Cloud Integration

 

The above iFlow modelling presents a reference for consuming messages from AEM using the AEM adapter and forwarding to a target system with “exactly-once” quality.  

To enable exactly once processing of messages, the following steps are needed: 

AEM sender adapter channel configuration

While consuming from AEM, in the AEM adapter configuration, the acknowledgement mode should be ‘Automatic on Exchange Complete,’ this is important since the adapter waits for the entire exchange to be completed and does not return a response immediately. 
 
 The consumer mode should be set to ‘Guaranteed.’  



The iflow needs to implement idempotent behaviour
 
This can be achieved by using local idempotent process call step. Once the message flows to Cloud Integration, the message ID can be set using groovy scripting.  
 
The approach defined above for setting message property can be used here as well.  
 
The reason for setting a message ID in the consumption iFlow is to provide some context to Cloud Integration to determine the uniqueness of the message. The duplicate check is performed with the help of the local idempotent process call. Before executing the local idempotent process call, Cloud Integration checks if the message ID has been processed before, if yes, it is not processed again. Hence, even if the client publishes the messages multiple times, the message would only be processed once. 
 
If the message has not been processed before, Cloud Integration will execute the local idempotent process call and thus, forward the message.  

It is up to the end user to build their own logic for determining the uniqueness of the message, in this case, the message ID was part of the message payload itself. 

Handling failures 
 
If there is a failure at the camel exchange level, e.g something goes wrong with the groovy script “setMessageID” as shown above or the HTTP call to S4HANA fails, the message would be retried as per the processing configuration of the adapter.  
 
As you can see in the above configuration, we are setting the ‘Maximum message processing attempts’ to 5, this means the adapter attempts the delivery five times.  
 
However, after the maximum attempts of the adapter, when the message is returned to the broker, the queue level configuration kicks in.  

If you have set the ‘Redelivery’ to ‘Try forever’ at the Queue level on the broker, the message is again picked up for processing by the adapter and this loop continues forever! 
 
However, there are other strategies of dealing with this –  
 
(i) You can set a finite redelivery count for your queue and then the message won’t block the queue throughput. The message would be discarded once the redelivery count is exhausted. 
 
(ii) It is also possible to use a 'dead-message-queue (DMQ)' in conjunction with the redelivery count on the broker. The message moves to the DMQ after the retry attempts are exhausted.  
  
There could be more ways of handling failures/retries but that this beyond the scope of this post.  

Ensuring order during consumption 

The “in-order” processing quality is ensured while publishing by waiting for acknowledgement before sending a new message. Fortunately, on the consumption side, since we are leveraging AEM adapter, the ordering is taken care of by the AEM adapter itself! (given that you have set acknowledgement mode to ‘Automatic on exchange complete’ and consumer mode to ‘Guaranteed’ as described above)  
 
The Cloud Integration worker nodes internally maintain a sticky connection to the Partitioned Queues which ensures that messages from the same partition are consumed in order.  
 
All of this combined in SAP Cloud Integration & AEM gives us the magic of “exactly-once-in-order” messaging. 

Scale with Partitions or Scale with Queues 

The ability to scale consumers effectively is a critical aspect of ensuring optimal performance and throughput. Since this blog concerns with “in-order” processing of events, we will be looking at a couple of strategies which can be used namely: 

Scaling with partitions: 

As discussed earlier, Partitioned queues divide message traffic into multiple partitions based on a partition key, which is defined by the producing client application. Each partition operates independently, allowing messages to be processed concurrently across multiple consumer instances. This parallel processing capability is essential for achieving high throughput and scalability in distributed messaging environments. 

Partitioned queues facilitate horizontal scaling by allowing multiple instances of a consumer application to process messages in parallel. Each consumer instance is responsible for processing messages from one or more partitions, enabling linear scalability as the number of consumer instances increases. 

It’s important to note that auto-scaling capabilities (e.g. Kubernetes Event-driven Autoscaling [KEDA]) can also be leveraged to automatically adjust the number of consumer instances based on workload demand. Cloud-based deployment platforms and container orchestration frameworks provide built-in support for elastic scaling, enabling consumer applications to dynamically scale up or down in response to changing message traffic patterns. Scaling policies and thresholds based on different criteria can be defined to implement proactive scaling strategies to anticipate workload spikes and ensure timely resource provisioning. 

Scaling with queues: 

In SAP AEM, multiple queues and pattern matching in subscriptions can be leveraged to achieve consumer scaling while maintaining in-order processing.  

Multiple queues can be used to distribute message traffic across multiple consumers, facilitating parallel message processing and horizontal scalability. Pattern matching subscriptions allow consumers to subscribe to multiple queues simultaneously based on subscription patterns, enabling dynamic routing of messages to matching queues. By combining these features, organizations can achieve both scalability and in-order message processing in complex messaging scenarios. 

To read more about pattern matching in SAP AEM, you can refer to the docs here: Wildcard characters in topic subscriptions 

To configure multiple queues with pattern matching, you need to create each queue separately and assign it to a specific consumer. Each consumer then processes messages from its assigned queue. The number of queues you create will depend on the level of parallelism you want to achieve.  

When a new consumer is added, a new queue can be created and assigned to it. If a consumer fails or is removed, its queue can be reassigned to another consumer or deleted. This dynamic assignment and reassignment of queues enable consumer scaling and ensure high availability. 

While multiple queues and pattern matching enable high levels of consumer scaling, there are some performance considerations to keep in mind. As the number of queues increases, so does the overhead of managing these queues. Therefore, it's important to find a balance between the level of parallelism and the management overhead. 

Sincerely hoping that this helps you & your organisation level up your integration game with SAP Integration Suite!

entry.title: Re: First Half 2024 Release: What’s New in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central?
entry.content: Please add position text as an option for the Org. Chart.  Job text is not always the same as position text and is not 1:1, but rather 1:n (job:position(s)).

entry.title: Re: Using Job Scheduler in SAP Cloud Platform [0]: Intro and Prep
entry.content: Hi Carlos, could you please specify here what "Note: the order of binding is relevant !" or "And remember that the order is important (order of creation and binding)" actually means to the user of this service?

entry.title: New Enhancement related to GST trial balance (Multiple GST Report)
entry.content:
Hi Gurus,

We have a new requirement from our client for GST based Trial Balance format, is it possible to customize the same in SAP S4 HANA? if yes please let me know how we can prepare the same.

Note: Required columns are G/L a/c, G/L A/c description, Debit, Credit, Balance.

 

 

Thanks & Regards,

Kingsu Sharma

SAP Consultant

entry.title: Re: In RMK when a candidate accepts the online offer, the...
entry.content: @i311049Thank you for the SAP note above- Looks like that note is being updated and improved. But in manage languages - I don't see what I need to do to update the verbiage.   What I am looking to do is edit the pop up message on RMK when the candidate accepts an offer.  Can the picture also be changed?  BTW, it is a great enhancement that we really like but wanted to see if we could change the verbiage and picture.

entry.title: Re: S4CO1: S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: Exercises
entry.content:
Hi @JamelJ 
If you face any issues during enrollment or practice in the learning system, you can request help at any time by access our Practice site https://practice.learning.sap.com/#/my-systems

There you will find on the right hand side the Contact Us icon

Please click on it and choose the communication channel that fits best.

If you request technical assistance and enter a support ticket, please provide the following information in your message:

  • Course identifier and collection code or system release
  • Contingent Code
  • Training system ID
  • Your user ID (name) and e-mail address
  • Detailed description of the issue with screenshots and any notification e-mails to help the support colleagues better understand the issue

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Where I can learn more about event based production c...
entry.content:
Hi @Kishore05 

Here are some learning resources you can find on aur SAP Learning platform

  • Introducing Event-Based Production Cost Posting - SAP S/4HANA Finance
    Learn about event-based production cost posting in SAP S/4HANA Finance.
  • It is also part of the several SAP S/4HANA Finance learning journeys 

The New in Production Accounting – Event-Based Production Cost Posting posted in our SAP Community introduces the concept of event-based production cost posting for product costs by order. this might be a helpful resource.

Kind regards
Margit

Great blog. Do you know what is the solution for S4HANA private cloud as we cant use AL11. Moreover files are written in Temse store so we cant have SFTP enabled for it too in my opinion.

 

Thanks

Saurabh

entry.title: Re: Exposing On-Premise S/4 Apps to SAP Build Work Zone: A Step-by-Step Guide

I would like to know if it´s also possible to expose on-premise custom transactions as Fiori Apps into SAP Build Work zone please.

Thank you,
Mauricio

entry.title: ABAP Curriculum -First changes available
entry.content:
The technology industry is changing at a rapid pace with growing demand for IT-skilled workers with a significant 23% increase by 2031. 

To support everyone in the ecosystem with the latest cloud skills, we transform the ABAP classroom training and also offer self-paced learning resources to upskill the latest solutions. As companies evolve, more and more specialized cloud skills are needed.

Be one of the pioneers and open up new career opportunities with future-proof ABAP cloud knowledge.  

The transformation of the classroom trainings will take place on the 30th June 2024.

The following 12 classroom trainings are affected by the transformation:

  • BC100 Introduction to Programming with ABAP
  • BC400 ABAP Workbench Basics - SAP Business Suite
  • BC401 ABAP Objects
  • BC402 Advanced ABAP
  • BC405 Programming ABAP Reports
  • BC410 Programming User Dialogs with Classic Screens (Screens) with the Elements
  • NET310 Fundamentals of Web Dynpro for ABAP and NET313 Floorplan Manager for Web Dynpro ABAP
  • BC414 Programming Database Updates
  • BC425 Enhancements and Modifications
  • BC430 DDIC
  • BC481 Interactive Forms
  • D75AW Delta ABAP Workbench SAP NetWeaver 7.0 to SAP NetWeaver 7.51
  • S4C81 Develop Extensions with SAP S/4HANA Cloud ABAP Environment

To ensure that you are up to date with the latest ABAP cloud and clean core capabilities, you can use the alternative courses for the transformed classroom trainings. The alternative courses are already available in the course descriptions in the Training Shop.

All changes, new learning options, and new learning resources for the ABAP area will be available at the end of June.

entry.title: ABAP Curriculum - Jetzt über die ersten Änderungen informieren
entry.content:
Die Technologiebranche verändert sich in einem rasanten Tempo mit einer wachsenden Nachfrage nach IT-qualifizierten Arbeitskräften mit einem erheblichen Anstieg um 23% bis 2031. 

Um alle im Partnernetz mit den neuesten Cloud-Kenntnissen zu unterstützen, transformieren wir die ABAP Präsenzschulungen und bieten darüber hinaus Lernressourcen für das Selbststudium an, um die neuesten Lösungen weiterzubilden. Mit der Weiterentwicklung von Unternehmen werden immer mehr spezialisierte Cloud-Kompetenzen gefragt.

Seien Sie einer der Vorreiter und eröffnen Sie neue Karrieremöglichkeiten mit zukunftssicherem ABAP-Cloud-Wissen.  

Die Transformation der Präsenzschulungen findet am 30. Juni 2024 statt.

Folgende 12 Präsenzschulungen sind von der Transformierung betroffen:

  • BC100 Einführung in die Programmierung mit ABAP
  • BC400 ABAP Workbench Grundlagen - SAP Business Suite
  • BC401 ABAP-Objekte
  • BC402 Advanced ABAP
  • BC405 Programmierung von ABAP-Reports
  • BC410 Programmieren von Benutzerdialogen mit klassischen Bildern (Dynpros) mit den Elementen
  • NET310 Fundamentals of Web Dynpro for ABAP und NET313 Floorplan Manager for Web Dynpro ABAP
  • BC414 Programmieren von Datenbankaktualisierungen
  • BC425 Erweiterungen und Modifikationen
  • BC430 ABAP Dictionary
  • BC481 Interaktive Formulare
  • D75AW Delta ABAP Workbench SAP NetWeaver 7.0 bis SAP NetWeaver 7.51
  • S4C81 Erweiterungen mit der ABAP-Umgebung von SAP S/4HANA Cloud entwickeln

Um sicherzustellen, dass Sie mit den neuesten ABAP-Cloud- und Clean-Core-Fähigkeiten auf dem aktuellen Stand sind, können Sie die alternativen Kurse für die transformierten Klassenraumtrainings nutzen. Die Alternativkurse sind bereits in den Kursbeschreibungen im Training Shop verfügbar.

Alle Änderungen, neue Lernmöglichkeiten sowie neue Lernresourcen für den ABAP Bereich sind mit Ende Juni verfügbar.

entry.title: Replication flow in DataSphere not showing the extraction enabled ABAP CDS views
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I was trying to get extraction enabled CDS view in DataSphere , using the replication flow. I am not able to see views like - I_PRODUCT or C_PURCHASEORDERDEX when using the replication flow in DataSphere. 

I do see it when using the export entities in DataSphere.

Please let me know if I am missing anything.

I did check SAP note - 3343889 , 3297105 and 2890171.

Thanks,

Vinay

entry.title: Re: COE# SA email notification

let me know how we can configure.

Regards,

Rishabh

entry.title: Re: BW HANA Sizing - Results interpretation /SDF/HANA_BW_...
entry.content:
Hello @FOA 
as you can see, the sizing Report shows that the System can hold comfortably in a 2 TB Node, as you shoud try to avoid to split into several nodes.

once the system grows regulary, try to consider to use Dynamic Tiering or nearline storage (NLS) as alternative for the historical data to keep the main memory in balance.

See also SAP BW NLS Solution and why SAP BW/4HANA?

best regards Roland SAP first-guidance

entry.title: Re: SAP Digital Manufacturing (DMe, DMi) 2302 - Release Highlights!

there is any official documentation about extractor API for DMi ?

 

Thanks!

Alberto

entry.title: Re: Can CPI return the response in.txt format?
entry.content: Define .txt file format... what is the actual format that is returned including the declared content type?

entry.title: Stories missing in Report Center
entry.content:
Hello everyone, 

my customer can create new stories and save them but after saving he can't see them in report center. 

He also doesn't see story reports that have been shared with him. 

When I run a check in Check Tool - User record exists for Stories in People Analytics and can see that there is an issue. 


Do you think this is the reason why the customer can't see stories in report center that he created? On the other hand if there is a sync issue why the customer is able to create new story reports at all? 

Best regards
LK

entry.title: Re: ILM Migration: change ILM WebDav Store Provider
entry.content:
@tilo-sap and @revathimp

you can use the onboard ILM WebDav Provider aka ILM Store as well.

have a look to the online Document - SAP ILM based on SAP IQ Database and you will see that SAP IQ is the key.

Best Regards Roland SAP first-guidance

entry.title: Help wanted - Multi-Currency in SAP Incentive Management
entry.content:
Hi all,

I hope somebody can help me on this.

I have sales reps in Canada that sell in USD and CAD and need to be paid in the respective currencies, so no conversion through exchange rates, therefore 2 deposits/payments, 1 in USD and 1 in CAD.

I tried to poll the "Unit Type" from the transaction in order to set up my rules to distinct between the currencies but I cannot poll this info for some reason in the formula editor. In the end I used the following workaround, which seems to work, but I don't believe it is best practice:

I'm using Event Types commission_USD and commission_CAD in the transaction. I have created 2 credit rules and 2 measurement rules that use that Event Type conditionally in order to separate between the currencies and create outputs for USD and CAD. Then I created 2 outputs on the incentive and deposit rule to do the same there. It does work, but I'm asking myself if there isn't a more best practice way of working multi-currency in a single plan.

Anybody can help me on this?

 

thanks a lot!

Joris

entry.title: Archival of old data in Solman(DB-Sybase / SLES)
entry.content:
Hi,
We are SOLMAN on Sybase 16.0.2 on SLES 
We want to archive old data to deal with recurring sizing issue
old data like Charm, basically usagae are - ChaRm, EWA, and ITSM
apart from this even we can archive other data as well 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Archival of old data in Solman(DB-Sybase / SLES)
entry.content:
Hello Customer,

 

Regarding the requirement "archive old data (like Charm) to deal with recurring sizing issue", from the Charm perspective, could you please check the KBA 2829393?

##2829393 - Can Charm and QGM documents be archived/deleted? - Solution Manager

 

Please contact SV-SMG-EWA for EWA questions and contact SV-SMG-SUP for ITSM questions.

 

Best regards,

Leo

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: In service cloud v2, case fields are not getting upda...
entry.content:
Hi @AnujaS ,

You need to pass the x-crm-token in the header in case of a patch.

 

Simon

entry.title: SAP iRPA Outlook Email Search Criterion - how to add OR Condition
entry.content:
Hi

Is there a way to add multiple Email Search Criterias but with OR condition? Currently, if you mention 2-3 search conditions, by default it takes them as AND conditions and the search happens accordingly. 

I need to add multiple search critera, but using OR condition. Does it allow such functionality? 

Thanks

Sandip

entry.title: Re: Implementation of Local Extensions for SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing (ICVCP3_EN_Col01)
entry.content:
For SAP-internal employees, if the learning asset link does not work, you are missing an authorization for the SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support. Please utilize the Internal Edition of SAP Learning Hub

Note:  First time users will be prompted to subscribe. The subscribe button is at the bottom of the page. Once you subscribe, you will get instant access to SAP Learning Hub, internal edition.

entry.title: Whats the Mac Shortcut key equivalent to CTRL+# in Windows (to comment out multiple lines)?
entry.content:
Dear all,

as already mentioned in the headline, I am looking for the Mac equivalent of "CTRL+#" to (un-)comment multiple lines in the SAC Coding. 

Thanks in advance,
Regards,
Chris

entry.title: Re: BRF+ data Validation
entry.content:
Hi,

Have you tried maintaining the primary key fields as mandatory? Also in the table settings under the decision table, you need to select the option of unique fields only for the particular column. Hope this should work.

Snedden

entry.title: Re: Public holiday not showing on generated work schedule...
entry.content:
Confirm the validity dates of your new Holiday in the Holiday Calendar and that you have regenerated the Work Schedules of the required grouping(s).  At this stage, your new Holiday should appear in the employee's work schedule, in the DEVelopment environment.

This process has generated a Workbench transport (the Calendars) and a Customizing transport (the Work Schedules), and both these transports then have to be applied first to your QualityAndTesting and then to your PRoDuction environments.

entry.title: recreate clustered indexes on huge table
entry.content:
Dears,

we have a huge table with a clustered index and 6 non-clustered indexes, when we create this index in the past there is one of our processes -which executes on this table- took as an example about 10 minutes to complete, after a week later , the same process took about 30 minutes, even though we run update statistics on that table at midnight everyday,

kindly note that table is the most active table in the database with a lot of insert commands and also a lot off select ones (the delete operation is prevented)

Now we are thinking  to drop the clustered index and recreate it,

so my inquiries are:
If we drop it, what the affect to the non clustered indexes on the table?
as the data in the table is already sorted, what about specify sorted data while creating the index in order to speed up the time of creating it?
the current fill factor value for all indexes in that table is 0, how to calculate the best value to change to it, and what are the benefits on both insert and select commands on the table

kindly note that clustered index is composed of 3 columns

 

Regards

entry.title: Re: Final Review Step in Onboarding 2.0 Process
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Has anyone experience that the Document flow is not triggering after the Final review even though it is part of the process variant? What's happening to us is that the Document Flow step does not appear again after the final review changes on PDC and ADC. Meaning, any changes made after final review, do not reflect on the form and signature step is not triggered. So the form now is not updated and not in sync with the updated information.

entry.title: Archiving old SXI_MONITOR messages
entry.content:
Hello wonderful people, I need some help.

We run a multi-client setup in SAP and are approaching our storage limit.
Checked DB02 and noticed that SXMSCLUR is take taking up a HEAP of disk space.

After going through various posts on this site as well as a few forums, I couldn't find a good answer for this -
I want to DELETE all messages in SXI_MONITOR, prior to 01.01.2022
Checked in the SXMB_ADMIN under archiving, but it does not allow me to select a specific date from which the messages could be deleted.

Am I missing something obvious?

entry.title: Transport Error (after-import method RS_ODSO_AFTER_IMPORT for ODSO )
entry.content:
I am trying to transport a new object (ODSO) from DBW to PBW and it is not working.

The transport error message:

Start of the after-import method RS_ODSO_AFTER_IMPORT for object type(s) ODSO ( )


Error when activating Objeto DataStore (clásico) ZOSIP1


Error when resetting Objeto DataStore (clásico) ZOSIP11 to the active versio

Start of the after-import method RS_RSDS_AFTER_IMPORT for object type(s) RSDS ( )

Start of the after-import method RS_TRFN_AFTER_IMPORT for object type(s) TRFN ( )

Start of the after-import method RS_ISIP_AFTER_IMPORT for object type(s) ISIP ( )

Start of the after-import method RS_DTPA_AFTER_IMPORT for object type(s) DTPA ( )
Transformation 0D1BRS9URSHLABQ2FKC0KWVG609EI3ZC inactive; action cannot be executed
Transformation 0D1BRS9URSHLABQ2FKC0KWVG609EI3ZC inactive; action cannot be executed
Error while saving Proceso transferencia datos DTP_00O2THN2VOW0AH4P6EOW2CP6K

Starting after import method RS_DTPA_AFTER_IMPORT for object type(s) DTPA in delete mode

Starting after import method RS_ISIP_AFTER_IMPORT for object type(s) ISIP in delete mode

Starting after import method RS_TRFN_AFTER_IMPORT for object type(s) TRFN in delete mode

Starting after import method RS_RSDS_AFTER_IMPORT for object type(s) RSDS in delete mode

Starting after import method RS_ODSO_AFTER_IMPORT for object type(s) ODSO in delete mode

Starting after import method RS_ROUT_AFTER_IMPORT for object type(s) ROUT in delete mode

Summary of objects with errors from request DBWK913506
Errors occurred during post-handling RS_AFTER_IMPORT for DTPA L
The errors affect the following components:
BW-WHM-MTD (Metadata (Repository))

entry.title: Condition Contract: Accrual capping, Intercompany postings possibility
entry.content:
Hi,

I have three question for conditions contract process (Settlement Management)

1. I have requirement to put capping for accrual. e.g Accruals should not happen once a specific amount is reached. Is it possible in standard?

2. Does intercompany settlement works under condition contracts? e.g: I have three companies A, B and C. Company C is selling goods to end customer where Company A and B would receive some commission. My approach is to set up separate condition contract under agent commission for A and B. At the time of settlement Company C will issue the settlement document but at the same time company  A and B should also post the incoming document against the company C settlement document like Intercompany supplier invoice happens with IDOC.

3. There is requirement that customer would issue and debit memo for rebate amount and system should post that incoming document against the condition contract as settlement document.is it possible?

entry.title: Integrating Standard Cost Estimate in Margin Analysis CO-PA using Report Painter (KE30)
entry.content:
Hi Expert,

How to flow Standard Cost Estimate(Sale Order Cost Estimate) in CO-PA Margin Analysis by using report painter KE35 and KE31 T Code.

I have added a cost element in the report painter form that is assigned to the cost component structure but the cost does not flow, how is possible.

entry.title: Re: Import Preconfigured Analytics Stories in IBP
entry.content:
Dear customer,

unfortunately the option is currently not available. It is only possible to get the Preconfigured Analytics Stories with creating a planning area by copying the sample planning area SAPIBP1.

Best regards
Yvonne
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: How do I map T162S-FAUS1 and FAUS2 values to their respective fields
entry.content:
Dear all

I think i found a way to map table T162 with the field labels but i'm stuck with the table indicator nr (see under) - but already a first step forward

table T162Y: Groups: Field Selection  ==> extract to excel

 

Table T162X: Description of Field Groups from T162/T162K ==> extract to excel

For each line you will have a position nr and a group nr. There's also a table indicator (1,2 or3) nr to take into account later on.

so exemple: field label "Plant" belongs to group '01' - group 01 in table T162Y is from selection group 'Basic Data, Item'

and is the first position from FAUST1

Extract T162 data to excel, this must be done correctly meaning that you will have 5 types of characters:

 -  .  *  +  and a space

From sheet1 copy column with field keys and FAUST1 to new sheet, paste

go to first free cell next to values and apply excel formula: =MID(E1;SEQUENCE(1;LEN(E1));1)

E1 =  cell containing the data

copy FAUST2 on the right of last column and proceed the same way with the excel formula.

do the same for the other columns

the excel formula will split the value and put each character in a separate column

give a sequence number to each column, not to the faust columns if you didn't delete them,

Notice that if a field from table T162 is empty you will have an error #Calc!, that's ok

with vlookup formula lookup the sequence nr you added in table T162X and apply for each sequence nr.

You will get the field label for each position. 

My issue: 

There's  'table indicator nr'  in T162X. (FAUTB) with value 1 - 2 - 3.

That indicator can a same position but for another group and field label

eg:

table ind nr 1  - pos 01 - description: plant - grp 01: basic data, item

table ind nr 3  - pos 01 - description: supplier - grp 20: basic data

for indicator 2 no link created to a grp 

any help?

Kind regards

Nicolas

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402.3: Final What's New & Product Assistance available
entry.content:
With immediate effect, the final What's New information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402.3 is now available. SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is based on the technical product SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

To access the Final What’s New information, please use the What’s New Viewer.

For further formats and languages of the What's New information, as well as pre-filtered views, please use the following SAP S/4HANA Cloud Help Portal page.

In addition, the final Product Assistance (aka Application Help) for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402.3 is available. Please use the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Help Portal page to access the Product Assistance in multiple languages.

For you as a customers, we recommend to also use the Release Assessment and Scope Dependency (RASD) tool.

The Release Assessment and Scope Dependency (RASD) tool provides you with a personalized and tailored view on What’s New information based on the activated and used scope within your system. The information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402.3 will be available in RASD within the next few days. A valid Customer S-user ID is required for login. We recommend to use RASD to prepare for upgrades and updates of your system and to focus on the new and changed features that are relevant for you as a customer.

entry.title: Re: How to enable an OData V4 service for anonymous access?
entry.content:
Nice Blog @Andre_Fischer . thanks for sharing.

One small note here, the Alias name in the URL in your blog is ZODATAV4, but the screenshots shows ZODATAV4_2, which is bit confusing.

Question:

Did I understood correctly that even for publishing multiple Odata V4 services over internet/intranet without any authentication requirement for the consuming client, we just need to add those services in that specific PFCG role assigned to that service user? and no need to create a new ZODATAV4*** Alias node for each Odata V4 service. is that right?

Another question : This Alias is like a hidden gem in SICF which only few consultants in the project will know about. so will SAP introduce a standard Alias to make this concept more generic in the near future?

Regards,

Bharat Bajaj

entry.title: Re: End-to-End Visual Inspection Process in SAP Digital Manufacturing
entry.content:
Nicely  written , many new things  in this - Superb.

Br,

dsk

entry.title: Re: SAP Field service management to BW/4Hana
entry.content:
Hi @dirk_meyer2,

From Datasphere's point of view, the supported source connections are listed here:
Integrating Data via Connections. There is no mention of FSM specifically.

I suggest checking FSM's documentation about how FSM exposes data, looking for some generic ways like: Generic JDBC, Generic OData API, Cloud Data Integration, Open Connectors.

BR.

entry.title: Unable to add an exteral URL in SAP Build apps using Inline frame
entry.content:
Hello All,

i am trying to add an external URL in one of the SAP Build apps using Inline Frame . So i am trying to add the URL in src properties but i am a blank screen while previewing the app in Web.

 

Please guide how to embed the URL.

entry.title: Re: ME51N check availability button
entry.content:
When create purchase requisition with item category ' ' or purchase requisition with item category 'U' by t-code ME51N, Click button "availability" in source of supply tab, the system doesn't have any response and doesn't display availability. Could you solved this problem. can you explain reason why no response.

 STO PR

standard PR

thank you

entry.title: Re: Can we change Customer Pricing Procedure (01) during...
entry.content:
Hi dear colleague,

In your case I think that the Modular Pricing Procedures for Automatic Inclusion of Price Conditions may solve your and fits to your requirement. Ill just provide a short explanation from a documentation and a link for it. please check if this approach is possible to be implemented in your case.

Modular Pricing Procedures for Automatic Inclusion of Price Conditions

You can use this function if you plan to set up pricing procedures that can be divided into a common part, that is valid across different business processes or areas, and a specific part, that is valid for specific business transactions. For this purpose, you can define either a main procedure that covers the common part and multiple subprocedures that reflect the specific parts, or the other way around.

 
Example

  • Business example 1: In a sell-from-stock process, the calculation of the net price can be the same for several countries/regions, but the conditions for tax determination differ.

  • Business example 2: Different sales organizations have different pricing strategies, for example regarding discounts, but the condition for tax determination is the same.

Kindly reach out more in the following links;
Modular Pricing Procedures for Automatic Inclusion of Price Conditions | SAP Help Portal
Automatic Inclusion of Price Conditions Using Modular Pricing Procedures | SAP Help Portal

Regards,

Julio Cesar

entry.title: Re: Payroll error (No entry in table T512W for key 99 2LE...
entry.content: In table T512W, 99 is the Country Code while 2LEP would be your Wage Type, and the message indicates that WT does not exist on June 30 2023 (30062023).  Such an error referencing County Code 99 usually happens when one of the employee's infotype is incorrectly linked to CC 99 or when the employee's assignment is incorrectly assigned to CC99 in a Feature.

entry.title: availability button no response in me51n
entry.content:
Dear expert:

       When create purchase requisition with item category ' ' or purchase requisition with item category 'U' by t-code ME51N, Click button "availability" in source of supply tab, the system doesn't have any response and doesn't display availability. Could you please check availability function. how can show this function of availability in source of supply tab, Require which setting in system.  Screenshot as below:

   Standard  PR 

STO PR

thank you

entry.title: Brand New GUY
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

                        You could say I'm brand new to this. My need is help with everything. I'm trying to figure out how to use Developersap.com but having great difficulties with it. Just trying to figure this out was a nightmare for me. I didn't give up and just kept pushing buttons and got here hopefully this is the right place. You could say I live in the New Orleans area because if I told you the town you would have no idea. Would like some friendly help about to get me started or pointed in the right directions. Thank you to all who read this and are willing to help.

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Business Intelligence Statement of Direction – May 2024 update
entry.content:
@EricFenollosa 
thank you very much for this summary 👍

One question regarding SAC:

I assume, you are aware that there are still several (important) gaps when you compare the features of the New model vs. the Classic model and the Optimized Story Experience vs the Classic Design.

Is there a dedicated roadmap to close all these gaps?

It feels like a never-ending story of "newly developed tools" which will never reflect all the capabilities the "legacy" tools offered. This is really sad. 😪

Only one small example out of hundreds: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/301001 ... from an end user perspective we don't understand why such "small things" obviously cause so huge changes?!

I think I'm speaking for the whole SAC community: Please focus on closing the gaps inside SAC and while doing this update these Influence requests to reflect the progress of long-desired features.

BR, Martin

entry.title: Re: How to check the connectivity to your backend system in Business Application Studio
entry.content:
Hi @banafshehalin 

Do you remember how did you find out about the problem about the on premise git url? Maybe checking the logs of the Cloud Connector

I'm getting the same error and I cannot find the source of the problem. I have 3 btp subaccounts and only happens in one of them.

Thank you,

Eva.

entry.title: System Fields Clearing after Document Add/Update
entry.content: I am experiencing an issue with fields in SAP B1 randomly clearing upon adding/updating new marketing documents.  I've seen it with the "Remarks Field", "Customer Ref. No." field, and different addresses under the "Ship-To" field on the logistics tab. Has anyone run into this before? I keep looking back at documents and realizing information is missing. Unfortunately it is quite random and I can't repeatedly replicate it.

entry.title: Replicating Opt-Outs from external webforms to Marketing Cloud (via CPI Integration)
entry.content:
Hello Community,

We have a question/issue in concern of replicating Opt-Outs from external webforms to SAP Marketing Cloud through CPI:

Background:
In our SMC multiple contacts exist with the same mail-address. Currently we are still using SAP MC Forms for the Opting-Out-process. If an Opt-Out for Email is submitted via these forms the optout will be saved in all of the contacts that have the corresponding mail-address based on the technical origin email.

Due to some technical problems in past, we want to be independent on these SMC Opt-Out forms, so that’s why we created an Iflow in CPI. The purpose was that an Opt-Out form now also can be created externally. The provider of this form only needs to send the necessary data to CPI like:
- Mail-address
- Marketing Area
- And a trigger which is responsible for the Opt-Out

Due to our Iflow in CPI the Opt-Out permission is saved in the contact in SMC with the corresponding mail address. This was already successfully tested from our side.

But:
If multiple contacts exist with the same mail-address we receive the following message in SMC import monitor:
“Multiple contacts found for ID EMAIL …”, see screenshot:


Now our question is: Do you know how we can solve this issue? We have to ensure, that this Opt-Out integration also works properly in case of multiple contacts with same mail-address.

Additional information: We are using the following API for this integration:
API_MKT_CONTACT

Thank you and kind regards
Sören

entry.title: System Monitoring metric "age of oldest bgRFC queue" how to check which is the bgRFC affected
entry.content:
SAP Solution Manager allows to run monitoring for bgRFC (Background Remote Funtion Call), 4 different metrics are provided:

  • Age of oldest bgRFC queue
  • Number of bgRFC queues
  • Number of bgRFC queues in error state
  • Number of bgRFC queues older than 1 day

Focusing on Age of oldest bgRFC queue, when data is retrieved in System Monitoring you will see only the numeric value in seconds for the oldest bgRFC, but running check data collection you will see a text indicating the corresponding bgRFC:

system monitoring bgRFC

With this information you can access corresponding Managed System, execute transaction SBGRFCMON and search for corresponding entry:

sbgrfcmon checks

entry.title: Re: Unable to load shared library '/home/site/wwwroot/libadonetHDB.dll' or one of its dependencies.
entry.content: Have you tried using the .NET SDK and the steps described at https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/hana-clients-dot-net-core.html ?  Note that the steps are slightly different depending on the platform you are on Windows vs Linux as is the download link from https://tools.hana.ondemand.com/#hanatools as you will need the appropriate client for Windows, macOS, or Linux.

entry.title: Re: 🔴 Integrate SAP API Management with 3rd party IDP for SAML/JWT/OAuth based Authentication
entry.content:
Hello @former_member54417 and @ajmaradiaga 

First of all, thanks for the really good content. We are actually facing a really similar scenario to the one explained here ( SNOW with SSO with Idp Azure Ad ) consumes a S4 on premise SOAP service via and API proxy exposed in SAP APIM. In our case we want to propagate the identity of the SNOW user to SAp S4. I have seen your video and presenation and it is good, but could you please give me more information about the policies you are using in SAP APIM?

From Azure Snow gets a SAML assertion as result of identification and I guesss we have to send this to SAp PIM, interchange there it for other kind of token generated in SAp PAIM and valdated by S4, right? we are using Cloud Connector too.

Can you tell me more information about what are the policies we have to use and how?please

Thanks

Jorge

entry.title: Re: Adding S4/HANA on-premise as source system in Identity provisioning service
entry.content: you have to assign "connectivity" service quota to directory as well if your subaccount is under directory, otherwise assign connectivity quota to subaccount, and subscribe to the service. create destinations to your backend systems in same subaccount where connectivity service is subscribed.

entry.title: Re: Common questions and scenarios in Higher Duty or Temporary Assignment
entry.content: @prasi55 That worked.  Thank you for fixing the link.

entry.title: Re: SAP CodeJam in Lisbon
entry.content:
Hello Thomas,

Thanks, if possible we would like to have two days, if possible.

BR

entry.title: Re: OData Services Functionality in SAP Cloud Version 2
entry.content: Thank you Tobias. With <hostname>/sap/c4c/api/v1/account*-service/accounts* , many others can be figured out.

entry.title: Re: Dynamically Reading Blobs from Azure Blob Storage to CPI
entry.content:
Hi  , Could you please help me at this error?

At HTTP adapter step, i am getting this error:com.google.common.util.concurrent.UncheckedExecutionException: java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: Status code:405; Reason:ï»¿

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>

<Error>

    <Code>UnsupportedHttpVerb</Code>

    <Message>

        The resource doesn't support specified Http Verb.

        RequestId:c750187c-a01e-003f-0b82-a752dc000000

        Time:2024-05-16T11:20:01.1919973Z

    </Message>

</Error>

@Nic_Frigius

entry.title: Re: Content Studio - Form Performance Reporting
entry.content:
Hello Bradly,

the corresponding CDS View is "I_MKT_ContentPageFormResult"
For field "ContentPageFormResultEvent" you need to use the correct value for the following KPIs:

OPEN Views
SUBMIT Submissions
SUBMIT_RATE Submissions / Views
NEW_CONTACTS New Contacts
NEW_CONTACTS_RATE New Contacts / Submissions

Br,
Jan

entry.title: Re: Business Technology Platform (BTP) Solution Diagrams & Icons - As seen at TechEd
entry.content:
Hi @siobhanmurphy @intiequals1 

I am not able to access the video link provided, please grant the access. 

Thanks,

Mainak

entry.title: Where is explained the Integration Center functions (operations) ?
entry.content:
Integration Center comes with a set of operations : 

 

  • Concatenate/Append

  • To Upper Case

  • To Lower Case

  • Truncate

  • Trim Whitespace

  • Remove All

  • Replace All

  • Prepend Text

  • Truncate from Left

  • Truncate from Right

  • Substring

  • Split

However, there is no explanation for them in the documentation : 

Available Operations Based On Calculated Field Data Types | SAP Help Portal

I would like to split email address in 2 fields : 

user for the section before @

domain for the section after @

I'm unclear weather i should use split or substring

entry.title: Re: Can CPI return the response in.txt format?
entry.content: Json format

entry.title: Re: Can CPI return the response in.txt format?
entry.content:
Just add a content modifier step at the end of your flow, and set the Content-Type header to text/plain.

 

Regards,

Ryan Crosby

entry.title: Error loading Fiori Application - App could not be opened
entry.content:
Dear SAP community,

we are getting an error while opening one Fiori App in FLP. App is exposed to Workzone via content federation, and was working previously. This is not a BTP custom App, but a UI5 app from S4HanaOnPremise, and from 3rd party. 
Same App is working in Test system. I checked the ICF / Odata services, also Autorisation, and everything seems to be fine. 
Has anyone experience something similar, and was able to resolve it? 

 

thanks,

Imtiaz

entry.title: Re: Transport Error (after-import method RS_ODSO_AFTER_IM...
entry.content:
Hello @kevin_portugues 
Can you please post a screenshot of the protocol, with all lines expanded?
In your log here, I cannot see the real root cause.
BR, Martin
